attrs.alt: Diagram for Persistent Memory Across Sessions

content.text: directory. Unlike Claude Code's native memory (which loads
at session start but forgets everything else), the Memory Tool enables Claude to
create, read, update, and delete files

: Structural instructions loaded at session start → conventions, architecture, business constraints
-

: Dynamic file storage → debugging insights, project state, session history
-
Claude Code has no native memory between two distinct sessions. At every startup, it forgets everything.
For your distributed systems work at Box Hill, this creates critical problems:
: Root cause discovered → lost next session → repeat debugging
: Architecture decisions forgotten → re-explain multiple times
: 100+ files without persistent context → agent can't continue

: Debugging insights survive → 39% task completion vs. 62% baseline
-

: Complete complex tasks instead of failing at turn 30
-
: You manage storage backend (filesystem, database, cloud)
Use native Memory Tool for production

Session 1: Start → Claude checks /memories (view)
         ↓
         Reads relevant files (view path)
         ↓
         During work → creates/updates files (create, str_replace, insert)
         ↓
        End session → files persist in /memories
         
Session 2: Start → Claude checks /memories (view)
         ↓
         Reads files from Session 1 (view path)
         ↓
         Continues work with previous context
Custom per-project setup, recent git log

1. Project CLAUDE.md + descendants → foundation
2. Personal global CLAUDE.md → cross-project preferences
3. Memory Tool /memories → session history
4. SessionStart hook (git log) → recent context
5. graphify (optional) → deep queries for large projects
Want Claude to remember what it did

# Project structure:
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md          # Global instructions (all sessions)
<project>/CLAUDE.md          # Project instructions (loaded when cwd inside)
<sub-folder>/CLAUDE.md       # Descendant instructions (loaded when cwd in sub-folder)
: Global → Project → Descendant (all loaded at session start).
Injects recent git log and session state into initial context.

# Install
npx claude-mem install

# Or from plugin marketplace
/plugin marketplace add thedotmack/claude-mem
/plugin install claude-mem

:
1. Captures messages, edited files, commands during session
2. AI-compresses into structured summary (10–30 lines)
3. Injects summary into next session's context

# Install
npm install graphify

# Initialize
graphify init /path/to/codebase

:
1. Builds typed, queryable knowledge graph
2. Exposes queries ("give me all places where function X is called")
3. Deep inspection for massive codebases

Claude automatically checks /memories:
{ command: "view", path: "/memories" }
→ ["auth-module-insights.md"]

Reads insights:
{ command: "view", path: "/memories/auth-module-insights.md" }
→ "JWT token expiry on line 847..."

Claude knows previous debugging without keeping 8,000 tokens

# /memories/project-architecture.md
## Microservices Architecture
- Auth service: JWT + refresh token rotation
- Payment service: REST JSON, cursor pagination
- Order service: Zod validation, async/await

## Coding Conventions
- TypeScript, not JavaScript
- Jest + Playwright testing
- 90%+ coverage required
Claude loads automatically → knows architecture without 100k tokens

# Project CLAUDE.md (foundation)
- REST JSON, cursor pagination
- Zod validation on server
- Jest + Playwright testing

# Personal ~/claude/CLAUDE.md (preferences)
- TypeScript, not JavaScript
- async/await over callbacks
- Write tests before implementation

# /memories/session-history.md (session history)
- Session 1: Refactored auth/ to async/await (8 files)
- Session 2: Added JWT validation line 847
- Session 3: Fix token expiry test (in progress)

# SessionStart hook (recent context)
git log --since="last week" --oneline
→ 12 commits: auth refactor, JWT validation, test fixes

# graphify (optional, large project)
graphify query "all places where auth.validateJWT is called"
→ 8 locations across 3 services

:
- Baseline: 62% task completion
- Context editing alone: 81% (+29%)
-

:
- Without memory: Fails at turn 30
- With memory: Completes 100 turns, 89% token reduction

:
- 100+ file TypeScript project
- Without memory: 185k tokens, fails
- With memory: 38k tokens, completes
-

:
- Use CLAUDE.md + claude-mem
- CLAUDE.md for foundations, claude-mem for session history
-
Claude reads entire directory → organize with specific names
: ZDR eligible → data not stored after API response
Claude Code has no native memory
For your Box Hill distributed systems work with multi-day debugging and large codebase refactors, I'd implement
together. This gives you the 39% performance boost while storing debugging insights in
, and recent git context via SessionStart.
